items.title: JDK 27 & Valhalla, Now! - Inside Java Newscast #113
items.summary: JDK 27 entered RDP1, Valhalla's JEP 401 is being merged into JDK main, join our hackathon, and more

items.title: Valhalla, Now!
items.summary: No OpenJDK project has excited Java developers as much as Project Valhalla and no other has strung us along for that long. But now the wait is over!

items.title: Avoiding Final Field Mutation
items.summary: Reflective mutation of final field mutation will soon be disabled by default and should be avoided if possible - here's how

items.title: You Must Avoid Final Field Mutation - Inside Java Newscast #110
items.summary: With JDK 26 / JEP 500 starting to prevent final field mutation through reflection, it is important that Java projects stop employing that practice.

items.title: Analysing Crashed JVMs - Inside Java Newscast #109
items.summary: The Java tool jcmd ("j command") sends diagnostic commands to the JVM, which, at the moment, requires a running JVM, but once candidate JEP 528 is adopted, a lot of the information can be seamlessly extracted from a crashed JVM's core dump, allowing easy post-mortem analysis

items.title: Java 27 - Better Language, Better APIs, Better Runtime
items.summary: Java 25 is the latest release with wide-ranging long-term support. It's a doozy and Java 26 and 27 followed hot on its heels.

items.title: Towards Better Checked Exceptions - Inside Java Newscast #107
items.summary: Java's checked exceptions are both an integral part of the language and one of its most contested features. Let's talk about specific issues with checked exceptions and what could be done about them.

items.title: Removal of ThreadPoolExecutor.finalize()
items.summary: With finalization being deprecated for removal, finalize methods are slowly being removed

items.title: LazyConstants in JDK 26 - Inside Java Newscast #106
items.summary: Lazily initializing fields in Java is error-prone and undermines constant-folding. JDK 26 comes with JEP 526, which previews LazyConstant , a type that lazily initializes a value through a given Supplier .

items.title: Carrier Classes; Beyond Records - Inside Java Newscast #105
items.summary: Carrier classes are a generalization of records that allow Java developers to succinctly define classes with a data-centric API that can participate in pattern matching and reconstruction

items.title: Java's Plans for 2026 - Inside Java Newscast #104
items.summary: In 2026, Java keeps evolving. Here's how the big OpenJDK projects Amber, Babylon, Leyden, Loom, Panama and Valhalla plan to push Java forward.

items.title: Java's 2025 in Review - Inside Java Newscast #103
items.summary: With 2025 coming to a close, let's summarize Java's year and look at the current state of the six big OpenJDK projects as well as a few other highlights.

items.title: All Features in Java 26 - Inside Java Newscast #102
items.summary: Java 26 enters rampdown phase 1, which sets its feature set in stone: HTTP/3 support, performance and AOT improvements, new command-line flags to manage final field mutation, and more

items.title: Java 26 Warns of Deep Reflection - Inside Java Newscast #101
items.summary: Java 26 will issue run-time warnings when a final field is mutated through reflection. This prepares a future change that will make such final field mutation illegal by default to improve Java's integrity.

items.title: Q&A About My Work At Oracle's Java Platform Group
items.summary: Answers to the remaining questions that didn't fit into Inside Java Newscast #100

items.title: Try the New Valhalla EA Build - Inside Java Newscast #100
items.summary: JEP 401, Value Classes and Objects, has recently re-entered "candidate" status and is getting ready to target a release.

items.title: Consistent Behavior of new File("")
items.summary: Prior to this change, a new File("") behaved differently, depending on whether it was queried as a relative or absolute file

items.title: Three G1 Improvements - Inside Java Newscast #99
items.summary: Java's (almost) default garbage collector G1 is undergoing even more improvements

items.title: All API Additions From Java 21 to 25
items.summary: Learn about all API additions between Java 21 and Java 25: scoped values, stream gatherers, class-file API, foreign function and memory API, Javadoc additions, and preview features.

items.title: Structured Concurrency in Action
items.summary: A deep-dive into the structured concurrency API as it is currently proposed

items.title: Upgrading From Java 21 To 25: All You Need To Know
items.summary: Updating from Java 21 to 25 is a smooth experience. Unless you're working on an unlucky project that collected all the little details that change: Whether it's annotation processing, null checks, file operations or the removal of old technologies, Peter collects them all.

items.title: Java Architects Answer Your Questions - Inside Java Newscast #97
items.summary: You asked questions, Java architects have the answers - from switch and if expressions to record identity, from optimal training runs to differentiable programming, and more

items.title: HTTP/3 in Java - Inside Java Newscast #96
items.summary: JEP 517 proposes to update Java's HTTP Client (introduced in Java 11) to be compatible with HTTP/3

items.title: Java Gets a JSON API - Inside Java Newscast #95
items.summary: Java considers itself a "batteries included" language and given JSON's ubiquity as a data exchange format, that means Java needs a JSON API.

items.title: Java 25 is ALSO no LTS Version - Inside Java Newscast #94
items.summary: Java 25, much like Java 21, will be described as a "long-term-support version" despite the fact that that's categorically wrong.

items.title: Java 25 Encodes PEM - Inside Java Newscast #93
items.summary: Java 25 previews an API that transforms PEM (Privacy-Enhanced Mail) texts into cryptographic objects like public or private keys, certificates, and certification lists and vice versa.

items.title: Java 25 Brings 18 JEPs 😱 Inside Java Newscast #92
items.summary: Java 25 will be released on September 16th. Its feature set has been frozen today and it is impressive: 11 finalized features in language, APIs and the runtime plus 7 that are brewing.

items.title: Structured Concurrency Revamp in Java 25 - Inside Java Newscast #91
items.summary: JDK Enhancement Proposal 505 revamps the structured concurrency API in JDK 25 by introducing a configuration and joiners.

items.title: JavaOne'25 Highlights - Inside Java Newscast #89
items.summary: JavaOne 2025 had lots of talks from OpenJDK insiders as well as from community experts - here are some of the highlights

items.title: New Null Checks in Inner Class Constructors
items.summary: The Java compiler will now inject null checks for the outer instance into the constructor of inner classes

items.title: Scoped Values in Java 24 - Inside Java Newscast #86
items.summary: Scoped values enable a method to share immutable data both with its callees within a thread and with child threads in a convenient, safe, scalable way, particular in comparison to thread-local variables.

items.title: JDK 24 Deprecates Remote Debugging with jstat And jhsdb For Removal
items.summary: Due to their reliance on RMI, which the JDK is moving away from, the remote debugging capabilities of jstat and jhsdb are deprecated for removal

items.title: Java Language Evolution in 2025 - Inside Java Newscast #84
items.summary: In 2025, the Java language keeps evolving. Here's how Project Amber plans to push Java forward: from flexible constructor bodies to simplified main, from module imports to primitive and deconstruction patterns, from withers to string templates.

items.title: Java's Plans for 2025 - Inside Java Newscast #83
items.summary: In 2025, Java keeps evolving. Here's how the big OpenJDK projects (Leyden, Valhalla, and more) sans Amber plan to push Java forward.

items.title: Java 24 Performance Improvements & Deprecations - Inside Java Newscast #82
items.summary: Java 24's feature list contains a whopping 24 JDK Enhancement Proposals. Here, we're going to look at the performance improvements and deprecations/removals.

items.title: JDK 24 Prepares Restricted Native Access
items.summary: JEP 472 prepares restricted access to native code through JNI and the FFM API

items.title: Java 24 Language & API Changes - Inside Java Newscast #81
items.summary: Java 24's feature list contains a whopping 24 JDK Enhancement Proposals. Here, we're going to look at the language and API changes.

items.title: Java 24 Stops Pinning Virtual Threads (Almost) - Inside Java Newscast #80
items.summary: On Java 24, virtual threads will no longer be pinned inside synchronized blocks, which increases ease of adoption

items.title: Release Your (Java) Projects Like OpenJDK With Tip & Tail - Inside Java Newscast #79
items.summary: "Tip & tail is a release model for software libraries that gives application developers a better experience while helping library developers innovate faster"

items.title: Advanced "Java 101"
items.summary: Java's success as one of the most used programming languages in the world comes in no small parts from its approachability but what may have been an easy entry into programming in the mid 90s, appears laborious today and so, in recent years, Oracle and OpenJDK have worked to improve the situation.

items.title: Big News from Project Valhalla - Inside Java Newscast #77
items.summary: At JVMLS 2024, project lead Brian Goetz presented the current state of Project Valhalla - this video summarizes the proposed changes to the programming model.

items.title: How to Read a JDK Enhancement Proposal - Inside Java Newscast #74
items.summary: OpenJDK evolves Java through JDK Enhancement Proposals, JEP for short, and uses them to communicate its intentions, but the the devil is in the details

items.title: Integrity by Default - Inside Java Newscast #73
items.summary: Integrity is a cornerstone of the Java Platform as it enables/bolsters reliability, maintainability, security, and performance, but there are operations that undermine it. Now, Java wants to lock them down by default.

items.title: Java and AI - A Structured Analysis
items.summary: What AI development needs and how much Java already has to offer, what it lacks, as well as what it's poised to acquire in the future

items.title: JDK 23 Removes COMPAT Locale Provider
items.summary: With the removal of the JDK's own (somewhat outdated) locale data, all projects must now use the CLDR data or other implement a custom locale data provider

items.title: Java and AI? - Inside Java Newscast #72
items.summary: AI development can be split into three categories: developing an ML model (where Java isn't competitive and is unlikely to become top of the class any time soon), developing an AI-centered product (where Java is well-positioned and will become stringer soon; but does this category matter in the long run?) and adding AI-based features to larger projects (whe…

items.title: Why Update Data-Oriented Programming to Version 1.1?
items.summary: A review of data-oriented programming version 1.1: What caused the update and what is still left to be improved?

items.title: What Happened to Java's String Templates? Inside Java Newscast #71
items.summary: JDK 23 doesn't contain the string template preview that was present in JDKs 21 and 22. Let's discuss why the feature was removed (probably not for the reasons you think), what a new proposal could look like, and when we may see it.

items.title: JDK 23 Changes Default Annotation Processing Policy
items.summary: Starting with JDK 23, annotation processors in the class path will no longer be executed without further javac configuration

items.title: When to use Data-Oriented Programming
items.summary: How does data-oriented programming compare to object-oriented and functional programming and what are good situations to start using it?

items.title: All Java 23 Features - Inside Java Newscast #70
items.summary: Java 23 will be released on September 17th but it's branched today (June 6th 2024) and so its feature set is final. Generational ZGC, Markdown in JavaDoc, deprecations in Unsafe , the removal of string template, and the thoughtful evolution of eight preview features. Let's take a closer look!

items.title: Separate Operations From Data - Data-Oriented Programming v1.1
items.summary: Data-oriented programming guides us towards a separation of data and operations. Operations should be implemented in dedicated subsystems, using pattern matching over sealed interfaces to pick execution branches and deconstructing records to implement domain logic.

items.title: Make Illegal States Unrepresentable - Data-Oriented Programming v1.1
items.summary: Data-oriented programming focuses on modeling data as closely as possible and a guiding principle for achieving that is to ensure that the software can not represent illegal states. That can be achieved with good type design or with constructor checks (plus tests).

items.title: Model Data, the Whole Data, and Nothing but the Data - Data-Oriented Programming v1.1
items.summary: Data-oriented programming (DOP) centers around modeling data as closely as possible and so a core principle of DOP is to 'model the data, the whole data, and nothing but the data'. This goal is best achieved with a mix of records and sealed types as well as some programming practices that may seem odd to the object-oriented developer - all of which is explo…

items.title: Model Data Immutably and Transparently - Data-Oriented Programming v1.1
items.summary: To model data immutably and transparently is one of the four principles of data-oriented programming. In this article, we explore why immutability and transparency are important when modeling data and how to use Java's features, particularly records, to achieve that.

items.title: Data-Oriented Programming in Java - Version 1.1
items.summary: Many of the language features recently added to Java come together to support data-oriented programming - a programming paradigm first described for Java in June 2022 by Brian Goetz. This here is a proposal for a revised version 1.1.

items.title: Module Imports in Java 23 - Inside Java Newscast #69
items.summary: To reduce the overhead of using APIs, particularly in single source files, Java 23 previews module import declarations of the form import module $moduleName , which import all packages exported by the named module

items.title: Java Withers - Inside Java Newscast #67
items.summary: JEP 468 proposes a solution to the verbosity that can come from modeling mutable state with immutable records: derived record creation aka with expressions aka withers .

items.title: Java 23: Restoring the Balance with Primitive Patterns - Inside Java Newscast #66
items.summary: The ongoing introduction of pattern matching to Java has unbalanced the language. Here's how primitive patterns (in Java 23) and other patterns (in future versions) will fix that.

items.title: JDK 20-23: Support For Unicode CLDR Version 42
items.summary: Java 20 updates its locale data to Unicode CLDR version 42, which comes with formatting and parsing changes, particularly of dates and times
